steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The words before the blank form a complete sentence about dark rooftops raising surrounding air temperature. The words after the blank form another complete sentence: Adding a light-colored covering helps combat the effect.

steps.title: Use a clear boundary
steps.content: Choice A uses a semicolon followed by by adding, leaving the second part without a proper subject. Choice B creates a comma splice. Choice D turns the sentence into the illogical idea that dark rooftops raise temperature by adding light coverings. The answer is C.
